pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng and verified in install_wireshark.sh script
Specified in task.json description and referenced in export_result.sh
Computed and stored by setup_task.sh at line 85-86
Counted from actual PCAP file using tshark frame.time_epoch field
Computed as floor(last_timestamp - first_timestamp) from actual packet data: floor(1595469951.905618 - 1595469924.23464) = 27
Maximum bin value from per-second packet distribution, occurring at second 9
First index where max(bins) = 17 packets occurred in 1-second interval starting at offset 9
Count of 1-second intervals with zero packets from 27 total seconds
Count of 1-second intervals where packet count >= 2 (burst_threshold)

privileged_info_summary: The PCAP file 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng is a real TCP capture from July 22, 2007, publicly available from the Wireshark sample captures collection. The file contains 35 total packets captured over a 27-second duration with an average rate of 1.3 packets per second. The peak traffic rate is 17 packets per second occurring at second 9, with 20 idle seconds and 7 one-second intervals meeting the burst threshold of 2 packets per second.
